title: Building an Observation Model (The A Matrix)

examples.title: Read a column from an A matrix
examples.description:
An A matrix is stored as a list-of-lists where `A_matrix[obs][state]` gives the probability of observation `obs` when the hidden state is `state`. Each column (all rows at a fixed state index) is a probability distribution over observations.

Complete the function so it returns the column at `state_index` as a list — one probability per observation.

tests.description: Returns the correct column for state index 0
tests.code:
A = [[0.9, 0.1], [0.1, 0.9]]
result = read_A_column(A, 0)
assert result == [0.9, 0.1], f'Expected [0.9, 0.1] but got {result}'

tests.description: Returns the correct column for state index 1
tests.code:
A = [[0.9, 0.1], [0.1, 0.9]]
result = read_A_column(A, 1)
assert result == [0.1, 0.9], f'Expected [0.1, 0.9] but got {result}'

tests.description: Returns a list with the correct number of observations
tests.code:
A = [[0.5, 0.5], [0.5, 0.5]]
result = read_A_column(A, 0)
assert len(result) == 2, f'Expected length 2 but got {len(result)}'

examples.title: Check if all A matrix columns sum to 1
examples.description:
Every column of a valid A matrix must be a probability distribution — meaning it must sum to 1. This is the same rule from Lesson 2 applied to each column of A.

Complete the one blank inside the loop so that `col_sum` holds the sum of all values in column `s`.

tests.description: Returns True when all columns sum to 1
tests.code:
A = [[0.9, 0.1], [0.1, 0.9]]
result = all_columns_valid(A)
assert result == True, f'Expected True but got {result}'

tests.description: Returns True for a mixed valid A matrix
tests.code:
A = [[1.0, 0.5], [0.0, 0.5]]
result = all_columns_valid(A)
assert result == True, f'Expected True but got {result}'

tests.description: Returns False when a column does not sum to 1
tests.code:
A = [[0.9, 0.2], [0.1, 0.9]]
result = all_columns_valid(A)
assert result == False, f'Expected False but got {result}'

tests.description: Returns True for a uniform A matrix
tests.code:
A = [[0.5, 0.5], [0.5, 0.5]]
result = all_columns_valid(A)
assert result == True, f'Expected True but got {result}'
